title: Configure PIM eligibility and Conditional Access MFA for Azure management
description: Practice making an Azure resource role eligible via PIM, activating it, and requiring MFA for the Azure management plane via Conditional Access.
azurePortalSteps: Entra admin center ▸ Identity Governance ▸ Privileged Identity Management ▸ Azure resources ▸ select a subscription ▸ add yourself as an Eligible assignment for the Reader role.
Activate the eligible assignment and observe the MFA/justification prompt, then confirm it appears under 'Active assignments' with an expiry time.
Create a new Conditional Access policy in Report-only mode targeting 'Windows Azure Service Management API' and requiring MFA; review the Report-only results after a day of sign-ins.

title: Register an app and configure a managed identity
description: Register an application, request a Graph permission, and compare it against a managed identity that needs no stored credentials.
azurePortalSteps: Entra admin center ▸ App registrations ▸ New registration; add an API permission (Microsoft Graph, delegated, User.Read) and observe the consent prompt.
Open Enterprise applications for the same app and review its service principal, then check Consent and permissions to see the tenant-wide user-consent policy.
Create (or reuse) a small VM or Function App, enable a system-assigned managed identity, and grant it a data-plane role on a Key Vault.

title: Build a network security baseline with NSGs, ASGs, and a UDR
description: Practice role-based NSG targeting with Application Security Groups, then force subnet egress through a route table.
azurePortalSteps: Create two test VMs in the same VNet, place each in its own Application Security Group (e.g. 'web', 'db').
Write NSG rules that reference the ASGs as source/destination instead of IP addresses, and verify connectivity behaves as expected.
Create a route table with a 0.0.0.0/0 route pointing to a test next-hop IP (Virtual appliance), associate it with the subnet, and use Network Watcher's 'Next hop' tool to confirm the effective route.

title: Add a private endpoint to a storage account
description: Remove public exposure from a storage account by switching from default access to a private endpoint.
azurePortalSteps: Create a storage account, then under Networking add a private endpoint for the 'blob' sub-resource, integrating a Private DNS zone.
Set Public network access to Disabled and confirm the storage account is no longer reachable from outside the VNet.
From a VM inside the VNet (or peered VNet), resolve the storage account's hostname and confirm it now returns the private IP.

title: Deploy Application Gateway with a WAF policy
description: Stand up a regional layer-7 load balancer with an active Web Application Firewall policy.
azurePortalSteps: Create an Application Gateway (WAF v2 SKU) fronting a simple backend (e.g. an App Service).
Create a WAF policy with the OWASP Core Rule Set, set Mode to Prevention, and associate it with the gateway.
Send a request containing a SQL-injection-like string in a query parameter and confirm it is blocked, then check the WAF logs for the matched rule.

title: Enable Bastion and just-in-time VM access
description: Remove standing exposure of a management VM by combining Azure Bastion with just-in-time access.
azurePortalSteps: Deploy Azure Bastion into a VNet containing a test VM with no public IP.
In Microsoft Defender for Cloud ▸ Workload protections ▸ Just-in-time VM access, enable JIT for the test VM and configure the RDP/SSH port, allowed source, and max request time.
Request access via 'Request access', connect through Bastion, and confirm the NSG rule closes automatically once the time window expires.

title: Configure storage data protection
description: Exercise the recovery and tamper-resistance features that protect stored blob data.
azurePortalSteps: On a test storage account, enable soft delete and blob versioning under Data protection.
Delete a test blob and restore it, then overwrite another blob and browse its version history.
Enable an immutability policy in time-based retention mode on a container and confirm you cannot delete a blob inside it before the retention period ends.

title: Configure Azure SQL security features
description: Layer identity, auditing, and masking controls onto a test Azure SQL database.
azurePortalSteps: On a test SQL server, set a Microsoft Entra admin under Microsoft Entra ID ▸ Set admin.
Enable auditing, sending events to a Log Analytics workspace, and generate a few test queries to confirm events appear.
On a test database, add a dynamic data masking rule to a text column and query it as a non-admin user to see the masked result.

title: Harden a Key Vault and create a governance policy
description: Combine Key Vault network/access hardening with an Azure Policy that enforces a security baseline.
azurePortalSteps: Create a Key Vault using the Azure RBAC access model, restrict its network to a specific VNet/subnet, and enable purge protection.
Assign yourself a Key Vault RBAC data role (e.g. Key Vault Secrets User) and create a test secret.
Create and assign an Azure Policy definition with the Deny effect that blocks public network access on new storage accounts, then attempt to create a public storage account to see it blocked.

title: Review Secure Score and enable a compliance standard
description: Explore Defender for Cloud's posture-management view and add a regulatory standard.
azurePortalSteps: Open Microsoft Defender for Cloud ▸ Secure Score and review the top recommendations for your subscription.
Open Regulatory compliance ▸ Manage compliance policies and enable an additional standard (e.g. ISO 27001), then review its control mapping.
Open Inventory and filter resources by a specific type to see how Defender for Cloud catalogs your environment.

title: Enable a Defender plan and try agentless scanning
description: Turn on active threat protection for a resource type and observe agentless vulnerability scanning.
azurePortalSteps: In Defender for Cloud ▸ Environment settings, enable Microsoft Defender for Servers (Plan 2) on a test subscription.
Confirm agentless scanning is enabled in the plan's settings, and wait for or trigger a scan cycle.
Review any vulnerability findings surfaced by Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management for a scanned VM.

title: Wire up Microsoft Sentinel automation
description: Onboard Sentinel and connect detection to an automated response.
azurePortalSteps: Onboard Microsoft Sentinel onto a Log Analytics workspace and connect a data connector (e.g. Azure Activity or Microsoft Entra ID).
Enable a built-in analytics rule template and confirm it appears under Analytics as an active scheduled rule.
Create an automation rule that runs a simple Logic App playbook (e.g. sends a notification) whenever a new incident is created.
